findings.detail: All 6 legacy files are confirmed deleted: src/app/(onboarding)/onboarding/[token]/OnboardingForm.tsx, src/app/(onboarding)/onboarding/[token]/page.tsx, src/app/api/onboarding/create-link/route.ts, src/app/api/onboarding/submit/route.ts, src/app/api/merchants/route.ts, src/app/api/merchants/[id]/status/route.ts

src/middleware.ts no longer contains pathname.startsWith("/api/onboarding/submit"). The allowlist correctly retains /login, /register, /onboarding, /api/auth, /api/health, plus the two regex entries for /api/templates/[id]/public and /api/templates/[id]/submit from Task 3.
prisma/schema.prisma: Merchant model is intact and unchanged. OnboardingToken model is absent (was already removed in Task 1, as expected). No schema changes were made in this task.

No TypeScript import references to the deleted modules were found in the codebase. The only remaining reference to a deleted module's runtime URL is a fetch string in StatusChanger.tsx (see finding below), not an import statement.

StatusChanger.tsx constructs a fetch URL `/api/merchants/${merchantId}/status` when `merchantId` is provided and `submissionId` is absent. This API route has been deleted. The component is used in src/app/(dashboard)/merchants/[id]/page.tsx (line 199) passing only `merchantId` (no `submissionId`), so status changes on the legacy merchant detail page will receive a 404 at runtime. Per the task spec this is acknowledged intentional legacy state — the merchant UI page is explicitly not being deleted. No action required; flagged for awareness.

The spec requires removing now-empty directories: src/app/api/onboarding/create-link/, src/app/api/onboarding/submit/, src/app/api/onboarding/, src/app/(onboarding)/onboarding/[token]/, src/app/api/merchants/[id]/, src/app/api/merchants/. Filesystem tooling was unavailable to verify directory removal; if these directories were not cleaned up they are inert (no route files, no side effects), but should be removed for cleanliness. The build tool will not error on empty directories.
